title: Result - Latvia
content:
Welcome to Latvia, one of Europe’s greenest countries, with 54% of its land covered in forests, 12,000 rivers and 3,000 lakes. Its 498-kilometre coastline along the Baltic Sea and Gulf of Rīga and major harbors like Rīga and Ventspils make it a regional transportation hub.
Visit Europe’s widest waterfall, Venta Rapids, or experience thrilling activities like bungee jumping in Gauja National Park. Immerse yourself in rich traditions through folk songs, mushroom foraging, or Art Nouveau architecture in Rīga. Latvia excels in exports like plywood, pharmaceuticals and chemicals, showcasing its innovation alongside its natural beauty and heritage.

## Unique food tradition
Jāņu siers (Latvian cheese): a bright yellow cheese, flavoured with caraway seeds.
The Latvian economy is largely based on service industries including transportation, information technology and financial services.

### Local legend or myth
Sun and the Moon: often personified in Latvian folklore as a married couple involved in a complex relationship.

### Language of the Land
Latvian is spoken by the ethnic population, known as Letts, who make up 64.3% of the population.
of Latvia is covered in forests, making it one of Europe's most forested countries and a nature haven.
